name: Request for Proposal
description: Comprehensive technical RFP / Scope of Work document for an enterprise Data, Analytics & AI platform implementation. Covers: platform objective, overall scope, detailed in-scope activities (data ingestion, lakehouse architecture, BI/KPI enablement, AI/ML readiness, DevOps, data governance, UI/UX, FinOps), out-of-scope, reference architecture, functional requirements by domain, non-functional requirements, implementation methodology (6 stages), project plan, helpdesk support, Annual Maintenance & Support (AMS), SLA definitions, breach & penalty framework, and data source appendix.
system_instructions: You are a senior enterprise architect and program manager drafting a detailed Request for Proposal (RFP) Scope of Work document for an enterprise-grade Data, Analytics & AI platform implementation. This document is addressed to prospective bidders and defines exactly what must be designed, built, and delivered. Write in formal, precise, third-person technical language — specific, unambiguous, and structured. Use active voice for requirements ('The selected bidder shall...', 'The platform shall...'). Functional and non-functional requirements must be concrete and measurable. Use Indian conventions for currency (Rs. Crores / ₹ Cr), dates (DD/MM/YYYY), and regulatory standards (DPDP Act, ISO 27001, DMBoK CMM Level-3). Architecture sections must name specific technologies (Azure, Databricks, ADLS Gen2, Power BI, Azure DevOps). SLA metrics must include specific thresholds and penalties. Do not use vague or aspirational language — every statement must describe a verifiable requirement or contractual obligation.

sections.title: Objective
sections.instructions:
Write the Objective section — 4-5 paragraphs describing the primary purpose and goals of this engagement:

- Paragraph 1: Overall goal — design, implement, and operationalise a unified, cloud-native Enterprise Data, Analytics, and AI Foundation Platform for AEML. Platform is scalable, secure, governed, and addresses the fragmented analytics landscape.
- Paragraph 2: Vision as a single trusted source of truth — standardised data ingestion, processing, and enterprise/domain data models across SAP and non-SAP systems. Self-service analytics, standardised KPIs, governed semantic models. Significantly reduce manual data handling, report duplication, and dependency on legacy analytics platforms.
- Paragraph 3: Modern lakehouse architecture objective — Azure and Databricks, Medallion (Bronze, Silver, Gold) layers, automated data quality controls, metadata management, lineage, observability, and RBAC. Support high-volume data (Smart Meter, operational data) and both near-real-time and batch analytical use cases.
- Paragraph 4: Future readiness — the platform must serve as the foundational layer for advanced analytics, AI/ML, GenAI, and Agentic AI in subsequent phases (enterprise control tower, intelligent automation). Must be modular, extensible, and cloud-native for onboarding additional business units without fundamental redesign.
- Paragraph 5: Overall outcome — robust, enterprise-grade data foundation that accelerates insights, improves data trust, strengthens governance, optimises efficiency, and supports AEML's long-term digital and AI transformation roadmap.

Note at end: 'The proposed Scope of Work is indicative and serves as a baseline. Detailed requirements shall be finalised during the Design Phase. AEML reserves the right to modify or reprioritise scope based on evolving business needs.'

Extract specific platform name, technology stack, and metrics from source documents.

sections.title: Scope of Work — Overall Scope
sections.instructions:
Write the Overall Scope section describing the full breadth of what the selected bidder is responsible for. Include:

- The engagement covers end-to-end establishment of the named platform: design, implementation, integration, testing, deployment, and support
- Cloud-first, scalable, secure, and governed foundational platform leveraging and extending the existing Azure and Databricks ecosystem
- Overall scope includes (as a bullet list): (1) Modern data platform and lakehouse architecture for batch and streaming ingestion, processing, storage, and consumption; (2) Data governance, metadata management, quality, lineage, observability, and stewardship aligned with DMBoK CMM Level-3; (3) Enterprise analytics and BI including semantic layers, KPI management, and self-service analytics; (4) AI/ML and advanced analytics capabilities including MLOps and LLMOps-ready frameworks; (5) Platform-level security, privacy, and compliance controls (RBAC, data masking, encryption, ISO 27001, DPDP Act); (6) Integration with identified enterprise and operational systems; (7) CI/CD pipelines, automation, monitoring, and operational controls; (8) Documentation, knowledge transfer, and training; (9) Post Go-Live Hypercare and AMS per defined SLAs

- Scope is limited to establishing the platform as a foundational capability. Business solutions (Digital Control Tower, GenAI/Agentic AI) are explicitly excluded.
- Bidders must propose future-ready, extensible solutions while strictly adhering to scope without assumptions on out-of-scope initiatives.

sections.title: In-Scope: Data Ingestion & Integration Foundation
sections.instructions:
Write the Data Ingestion & Integration Foundation in-scope section. Introduce with 1-2 sentences: 'The selected bidder shall design and implement a standardised, scalable, and governed data ingestion framework capable of onboarding both SAP and non-SAP systems (refer Appendix A1), supporting current and future enterprise and OT data sources.'

Then list as bullets what this includes:
- Integration with SAP systems (e.g., SAP ECC/S4, SAP BPC, SAP Datasphere, SAP SLT or equivalent) for structured enterprise data
- Integration with non-SAP systems including databases, files, APIs, IoT/OT platforms (SCADA, EMS, OMS), and third-party applications
- Support for batch and streaming ingestion patterns with configurable latency, throughput, retry, and recovery mechanisms
- Large-scale historical data migration/backfilling for defined time horizons for initial platform validation
- Any modification, customisation, or configuration of existing SAP systems (AESL SAP, BPC, BODS & Datasphere/SLT) as applicable to ingestion
- Built-in data validation, reconciliation, exception handling, and monitoring as part of ingestion pipelines
- Metadata capture, lineage tracking, and auditability for all ingested datasets

Close with: 'The ingestion framework shall be designed as a reusable, configurable platform capability — not as point-to-point integrations.'

sections.title: In-Scope: Data Storage, Modeling & Lakehouse Architecture
sections.instructions:
Write the Data Storage, Modeling & Lakehouse Architecture in-scope section. Introduce with 1-2 sentences on designing and implementing a modern, cloud-native lakehouse architecture leveraging Azure Databricks.

Bullet list of what the architecture shall include:
- Implementation of Bronze, Silver, and Gold data layers for data segregation based on ingestion state, quality, readiness, and consumption patterns
- Design and implementation of enterprise-level and domain-aligned data models for analytics, reporting, and AI/ML use cases
- Creation of curated, analytics-ready datasets optimised for BI tools, self-service analytics, and advanced analytical workloads
- Support for raw data preservation alongside business-friendly transformed datasets with source-to-consumption traceability and auditability
- Implementation of dataset versioning and lifecycle management including schema evolution, controlled changes, archival, and retirement
- Data modeling and lakehouse design ensuring consistency, reusability, and extensibility for future semantic layers, AI/ML, and agent-based use cases without fundamental rework

**Redesign/Refine Existing Data Model:** Sub-section. The selected bidder shall review, redesign, or refine existing data models already available in the Azure Databricks environment (implemented under previous or parallel vendor scopes) to align with the DRISHTI target lakehouse architecture, governance standards, and analytical requirements. Includes schema rationalization, naming conventions, relationship definitions, and data quality improvements without introducing source system dependencies.

sections.title: In-Scope: Self-Service BI, KPI Enablement & AI/ML Readiness
sections.instructions:
Write two sub-sections:

**Self-Service BI & KPI Enablement (Foundation Validation):** Introduce as 'Part of validating DRISHTI platform readiness, the selected bidder shall enable self-service BI capabilities for business users.' Bullet list: (1) Implementation of a governed semantic layer to define, manage, and expose business KPIs; (2) Enablement of role-based access for business users to explore, query, and visualise data using self-service BI tools (e.g., Power BI Embedded); (3) Development of a limited set of representative KPIs (~50, to be identified during Requirement Analysis phase) and dashboards for: validating data ingestion/modeling/governance, demonstrating performance/scalability/usability, testing KPI lifecycle management. State: 'The intent is not to deliver comprehensive business dashboards, but to prove the effectiveness and readiness of the data foundation platform.'

**AI/ML, GenAI & Agentic AI Readiness Enablement:** Introduce as 'The selected bidder shall enable platform-level capabilities required to support current and future AI/ML, GenAI, and Agentic AI use cases.' Bullet list: (1) Enablement of AI/ML development environments for data scientists and analysts; (2) MLOps and LLMOps-ready frameworks including experiment tracking, model registry, versioning, and monitoring; (3) Support for feature engineering, training, validation, deployment, and inference workflows; (4) GenAI and Agentic AI readiness including API-driven access, semantic data exposure, and secure data consumption patterns. Add: 'The selected bidder shall design, develop, and deliver 1 forecasting model and 1 prediction model for validating the platform AI/ML enablement and operational ML workflows. Specific use cases and success criteria shall be finalised during Requirement Analysis.'

sections.title: In-Scope: DevOps, Data Governance, UI/UX & FinOps
sections.instructions:
Write four sub-sections:

**DevOps Enablement:** The selected bidder shall establish DevOps and CI/CD capabilities to support automated build, test, and deployment of data pipelines, configurations, analytics artefacts, and AI/ML assets across environments. The DevOps framework shall support version control, environment promotion, rollback, and controlled releases, ensuring consistency, traceability, and faster iteration while maintaining governance and compliance controls.

**Data Governance, Stewardship & Compliance Enablement:** Introduce as a strong emphasis area. Bullet list: (1) Definition and implementation of data ownership, stewardship, and custodianship models; (2) Refinement and standardisation of Business Glossary and Business Taxonomy for datasets onboarded under the platform; (3) Enablement of metadata management including business and technical metadata; (4) Implementation of data lineage, data quality frameworks, and observability metrics; (5) Configuration of data access controls, classification, masking, and audit logging; (6) Alignment with DMBoK CMM Level-3 maturity, ISO 27001, DPDP Act, and internal audit requirements; (7) Establishment of standard operating procedures (SOPs) and governance workflows. State: 'Governance capabilities shall be embedded into the platform and processes, not treated as standalone or manual activities.'

**DRISHTI UI/UX:** The selected bidder shall ensure the platform provides a consistent, intuitive, and role-based user experience for business users, data stewards, data engineers, data scientists, and administrators, facilitating efficient data discovery, analytics, and platform operations. The platform UI/UX shall support ease of navigation, usability, and accessibility across key capabilities.

**FinOps:** The selected bidder shall enable FinOps practices during and after platform implementation to provide visibility into cloud resource usage, compute and storage consumption, and cost drivers. The solution shall support cost monitoring, optimisation recommendations, and usage transparency, enabling AEML to govern cloud spend effectively while balancing performance, scalability, and cost efficiency.

sections.title: Deliverables under Scope
sections.instructions:
List all deliverables the selected bidder must provide under this RFP as a comprehensive bullet list:

- Fully functional enterprise data foundation platform deployed in production
- Target architecture and solution design artefacts (HLD, LLD, architecture documents)
- Standardised data ingestion, data engineering, data modeling, and data science frameworks
- Enterprise and domain-aligned data models including curated Bronze, Silver, and Gold layer datasets optimised for analytics, BI, and AI/ML workloads
- Governed datasets and semantic models aligned with agreed business definitions, access controls, and governance policies
- Limited KPIs (~50) and dashboards for platform validation
- Delivery of identified AI/ML models (1 forecasting model + 1 prediction model) as defined under this RFP
- AI/ML, GenAI, and Agentic AI readiness capabilities including MLOps/LLMOps-ready frameworks, APIs, security controls, and architectural foundations
- Established data governance and stewardship enablement including business glossary and taxonomy refinement, metadata management, data lineage, data quality frameworks, and governance workflows
- DevOps and CI/CD pipelines for data engineering, analytics, and AI/ML artefacts
- UI/UX enablement for the platform
- FinOps enablement artefacts including cost visibility dashboards, consumption metrics, and optimisation recommendations
- Accelerators and reusable assets: data ingestion templates, data quality rules, validation frameworks, monitoring utilities
- Comprehensive documentation: solution architecture, data models, ingestion pipelines, governance processes, SOPs, and operational runbooks
- Training and knowledge transfer for designated AEML business, data, and IT teams

sections.title: Out of Scope
sections.instructions:
Write the Out-of-Scope section clearly stating what is explicitly excluded to avoid ambiguity. Organise by category with sub-bullets:

**Business Application & Source System Changes:**
- Any modification, customisation, enhancement, or configuration of existing source systems (SAP, SCADA, OMS, GIS, CRM, or other enterprise/OT systems) not explicitly mentioned in the Scope of Work
- Development or implementation of new transactional or operational systems
- Data correction, cleansing, or remediation at source system level beyond defined ingestion validation rules

**Full-Scale Business Reporting & Dashboard Development:**
- Development of comprehensive, department-specific, or enterprise-wide dashboards for all business functions
- Ongoing creation or enhancement of business dashboards beyond the limited KPIs (~50) explicitly defined for platform validation

**Digital Control Tower (Full-fledged Implementation):**
- Full-fledged Digital Control Tower implementation beyond the ~50 KPIs scope shall be addressed under a subsequent phase and separate RFP

**GenAI, Agentic AI & Advanced AI Use Cases:**
- Development or deployment of production-grade GenAI, conversational AI, chatbot, or Agentic AI solutions
- Training, fine-tuning, or deployment of Large Language Models (LLMs) for enterprise use
- AI-driven decision automation, autonomous agents, or closed-loop operational AI systems
- This RFP includes only platform readiness and architectural enablement for such use cases

**Licensing, Third-Party Products & Infrastructure Procurement:**
- Procurement of cloud subscriptions, software licences, third-party tools, or infrastructure unless explicitly stated in the commercial section
- Cost of licences for BI tools, AI models, or connectors unless included in the bidder's commercial proposal

sections.title: Reference Architecture (To-Be)
sections.instructions:
Write the Reference Architecture section describing the target-state architecture:

**Architecture Overview (prose):** The target state architecture is Cloud-First, cloud-native, leveraging Microsoft Azure and Azure Databricks as the core technology stack. It establishes a scalable, secure, and governed Enterprise Data, Analytics, and AI Foundation Platform. Builds upon the existing Azure and Databricks ecosystem (Azure Web Apps, ADLS Gen2). No parallel or alternative data platforms are envisaged in Phase I — focus is standardisation, consolidation, and strengthening of the existing cloud foundation.

**Core Architectural Principles (bullets):**
- Cloud-First and Managed Services-First approach using Azure native and Databricks managed services
- Lakehouse Architecture using Medallion (Bronze, Silver, Gold) pattern on ADLS Gen2 and Databricks
- Federated yet governed multi-BU model — shared platform assets with physical data segregation
- Security-by-design and governance-by-default aligned with enterprise and regulatory requirements
- Scalable and extensible design supporting future BU onboarding, data sources, and AI use cases without re-architecture

**Logical Architecture Components (describe each layer with a bullet list):**
- Data Ingestion Layer: Unified ingestion (SAP and non-SAP, batch and near-real-time, structured/semi-structured/unstructured, Smart Meter and high-volume OT data readiness)
- Data Storage & Processing Layer: ADLS Gen2 as enterprise data lake; Databricks for distributed processing (Python and Spark); Medallion architecture; domain-aligned data models
- Analytics & Consumption Layer: Semantic models for self-service BI; Power BI Embedded for dashboards; sub-5-second response for standard dashboards
- AI/ML & Advanced Analytics Layer: Databricks Model Serving; integration with OpenAI and enterprise GenAI services; MLOps patterns (Code-as-Deployment, Model-as-Deployment, CI/CD-driven lifecycle management)
- Application & Integration Layer: Node.js-based web applications on Azure Web Apps; secure APIs; event-driven and service-based integration patterns
- Security, Governance & Compliance: RBAC across Azure and Databricks; user/field-level/transaction-level access controls; column-level masking and encryption; centralised metadata, lineage, and stewardship
- Operations, Monitoring & Reliability: Automated job scheduling and orchestration; backup and recovery per enterprise RTO/RPO; monitoring via Azure Application Insights; pipeline/workload/user observability
- CI/CD & DevOps: Azure DevOps with GitHub Enterprise; Infrastructure-as-Code; environment isolation (Dev, Test, UAT, Prod)
- Multi-BU Enablement: BU-wise Azure Resource Groups; common platform services with physical data segregation; federated yet standardised approach for additional AESL BU onboarding

**Target State Alignment:** DRISHTI architecture serves as the foundational layer for: Enterprise analytics and KPI standardisation; Digital Control Tower (Phase II); GenAI and Agentic AI platforms (Phase III). Architecture must enable these without structural changes.

sections.title: Key Functional Requirements — Data Platform (Ingestion, Storage, Modeling, Quality)
sections.instructions:
Generate the functional requirements table for the data platform layer. Format as markdown pipe table:

| Sr. | Category | Requirement | Priority |
|-----|----------|-------------|----------|

For Data Ingestion & Integration (8-10 rows):
- Batch and streaming data ingestion support
- Multi-source connectivity (databases, files, APIs, IoT/OT, streams)
- Multiple file and table formats (JSON, Excel, CSV, XML, Parquet, Delta Lake)
- Data quality and integrity enforcement at ingestion
- Exception handling, alerting, recovery, and resume without data loss
- Historical data archival and backfill loading
- Integration with downstream data processing pipelines and workflows
- Metadata capture and lineage tracking at ingestion

For Data Storage & Processing (7-9 rows):
- Centralised storage with massive parallel processing (MPP) capabilities
- Horizontal scalability for volume and throughput variation
- Low latency and high throughput data storage and retrieval
- Data replication and backup for zero data loss
- Fault tolerance for hardware and software failures
- Data lifecycle management with version rollback capability
- Cost-effective storage with transparent cost management and monitoring

For Data Modeling & Semantic Layer (4-5 rows):
- Data normalisation, de-normalisation, and format standardisation
- Multi-source data integration and schema conflict resolution
- Bronze/Silver/Gold layer segregation per data quality and consumption patterns
- Governed semantic models aligned with business definitions for self-service BI consumption

For Data Quality Management (6-8 rows):
- Configurable data quality checks at multiple levels per DMBoK principles
- Checksum and hash verification for data integrity
- Data cleansing for errors, inconsistencies, and missing values
- Data transformation: aggregation, filtering, sorting, pivoting
- Data enrichment and data reduction capabilities
- Data quality dashboards, scoring, and reporting

All rows: Priority column = 'Must Have' for core requirements.

sections.title: Key Functional Requirements — Analytics, BI & Data Governance
sections.instructions:
Generate the functional requirements table for analytics, self-service BI, and data governance. Format: Sr. | Category | Requirement | Priority.

For Self-Service BI & Analytics Enablement (8-10 rows):
- Easy, secure, and governed access to enterprise data for analysis and visualisation
- Self-service reporting and ad-hoc querying with strict access controls
- Interactive dashboards and reports aligned to business-defined KPIs across multiple dimensions and time periods
- Role-based and persona-driven dashboards by BU, function, geography, and hierarchy
- Semantic and governed analytics layer for consistent KPI/metric definitions across all reports
- Centralised data and analytics foundation for multiple control tower views
- Enterprise-level, BU-level, and function-specific monitoring and visualisation (Grid, Retail, Operations)
- Standardised, reusable KPIs and analytical datasets for control tower and operational analytics
- Enterprise-wide visibility through centrally managed analytics with decentralised consumption

For Data Governance, Stewardship & Metadata Management (8-10 rows):
- Metadata management including data schema, dictionaries, and data catalog
- System performance monitoring (latency, throughput, resource utilisation)
- Compliance with data governance policies, privacy laws, retention policies, and access controls
- Data usage tracking, impact analysis, and data asset valuation reporting
- Ontology and taxonomy repository maintenance with entity relationship mapping
- DMBoK CMM Level-3 compliance
- Controlled schema evolution with backward and forward compatibility checks
- Automated metadata capture and management
- End-to-end data lineage across ingestion, processing, and consumption layers

sections.title: Key Functional Requirements — Security, Compliance & Platform Operations
sections.instructions:
Generate the functional requirements table for security, compliance, platform operations, monitoring, and scalability. Format: Sr. | Category | Requirement | Priority.

For Security, Access Control & Compliance (10-12 rows):
- Safeguarded from data breaches; compatible with AEML and Adani Group cybersecurity policies
- Controlled access and data sharing across all proposed services with management policies
- Row-level and column-level data filtering; sensitive data masking
- Encryption at rest and in transit using industry-standard mechanisms
- ISO 27001 security compliance
- DPDP Act privacy compliance
- RBAC for data, functions, and platform resources
- Fine-grained access control at dataset, column, and transaction levels
- Secure authentication and authorisation integrated with enterprise identity systems
- Detailed audit logs for data access, changes, and administrative activities
- No unapproved security tools; all tooling per Adani Group-approved configurations
- Support for periodic security audits, compliance assessments, and regulatory requirements

For Platform Operations, Monitoring, Alerting & Logging (6-8 rows):
- Configurable notifications and alerts for pipeline failures, SLA breaches, and anomalies
- Automated monitoring, root cause analysis, data lineage, and data health insights (data observability pillars)
- Exception handling, unavailability detection, recovery, and resume without data loss
- Centralised logging system: generate, manage, store, view, and validate logs
- Audit logs with specific retention policy
- Operational dashboards for real-time platform visibility, usage, performance, and failures

For Scalability, Elasticity, Availability, Reliability & Resilience (8-10 rows):
- Auto-scale up/down for varying user load and traffic with optimal resource use
- High availability with reliable data access and minimal downtime
- Fault tolerance for hardware and software failures without data loss
- Data replication and automated backup mechanisms
- Independent scaling of storage and compute
- Handle data volume and throughput variations
- Multi-tenant capabilities for new business units
- Support 200+ data source integrations without architectural redesign
- Environment isolation between Dev, Test, Staging, and Production

sections.title: Key Functional Requirements — AI/ML, Advanced Analytics & DevOps
sections.instructions:
Generate the functional requirements table for AI/ML, advanced analytics, and DevOps. Format: Sr. | Category | Requirement | Priority.

For AI/ML, GenAI & Advanced Analytics Enablement — Platform Readiness (14-16 rows):
- Data prep & feature readiness: preprocessing/cleaning/transformation tools; multiple data type and format support; annotation/labelling for supervised learning
- Experimentation: data versioning for tracking changes over time; experiment tracking to record and compare model configurations and runs; version control with RBAC for team collaboration; experiment reproducibility
- Model development: AutoML for automated preprocessing, feature engineering, model selection; manual and automated hyperparameter tuning
- Packaging, deployment & inference: deploy models as APIs/microservices/serverless functions; containerised deployment (Docker); batch inference on large datasets; inference error/exception capture; local/on-prem/cloud GPU model building as part of MLOps
- Monitoring & controlled releases: continuous model monitoring (performance, accuracy, latency in production); data/concept drift detection; automated retraining pipelines; canary releases for production testing; A/B testing for model comparison
- Registry, governance & Responsible AI: model registry with version management and rollback; model interpretation, feature importance, and bias detection; data privacy/model security/compliance monitoring; cost monitoring for training/deployment/inference; MLOps/LLMOps/DevOps lifecycle automation; Responsible AI framework (bias detection, fairness scoring, model risk classification, Human-in-the-Loop rules)

For DevOps / SDLC Quality Controls (5-6 rows):
- Automated unit tests, QA, and code quality enforcement tools
- CI/CD pipelines for code, configurations, and data workflows
- Version control integration (GitLab/GitHub Enterprise)
- Automated testing and deployment across environments
- Infrastructure-as-Code and environment standardisation
- Controlled artifact promotion across Dev, Test, and Production environments

For Multi-BU, Multi-Vendor Architecture, Governance & Collaboration (5-6 rows):
- Configuration-driven BU onboarding (metadata-, policy-, and template-based) rather than custom code development
- Multi-vendor operating model support with independent development/test/deploy without conflict
- Separation of responsibilities via standardised APIs, data contracts, CI/CD pipelines, and governance guardrails
- Enterprise-owned, vendor-agnostic, reusable platform assets (pipelines, models, semantic layers, metadata, documentation)
- Open standards and documented APIs to avoid proprietary vendor lock-in

sections.title: Non-Functional Requirements
sections.instructions:
Generate the Non-Functional Requirements as a comprehensive markdown pipe table:

| Sr. | NFR Category | Requirement | Target / Threshold |
|-----|-------------|-------------|-------------------|

Generate rows covering the following NFR categories with specific measurable targets:

**Performance & Responsiveness:**
- Average query/dashboard response time for standard BI: < 5 seconds under normal load
- Dashboard performance under 2× peak concurrent user load: no more than 10% degradation
- Performance monitoring for ingestion, processing, and consumption layers: Mandatory

**Ingestion Throughput & Latency:**
- Batch pipeline completion within defined batch window: Per pipeline SLA defined during design
- Streaming/near-real-time ingestion latency: ≤ 15 minutes end-to-end
- Retry and recovery: Automated without data loss

**Scalability & Elasticity:**
- Horizontal scalability for data volume, velocity, and user growth: Cloud-native
- Elastic compute scaling based on workload demand: Supported
- Additional AESL BU onboarding without significant redesign: Configuration-driven
- Independent scaling of storage and compute: Required

**Availability, Reliability & Resilience:**
- Monthly platform uptime (core components): ≥ 99.95%
- Data pipeline successful execution rate per month: ≥ 99.95%
- RTO: ≤ 60 minutes
- RPO: ≤ 30 minutes
- Automated failure detection, job recovery, and restart: Mandatory
- Environment isolation (Dev/Test/UAT/Prod): Mandatory

**Security & Access Control:**
- RBAC enforcement: Mandatory across all layers
- Data encryption at rest: AES-256 or equivalent
- Data encryption in transit: TLS 1.2+ mandatory
- Audit log retention: Per AEML policy
- VAPT clearance before Production Go-Live: Mandatory (CERT-In empanelled agency or as agreed)
- Critical security patch application: Within 48 hours of identification
- High security patch application: Within 7 days
- Medium/Low security patch application: Within 1 month

**Data Governance & Quality:**
- Data quality score for critical datasets: ≥ 98% (valid records ÷ total records per agreed DQ rules)
- Critical DQ failure resolution: ≤ 8 business hours
- Controlled schema evolution with backward/forward compatibility checks: Mandatory
- DMBoK CMM Level-3 alignment: Mandatory

**Interoperability & Integration:**
- Standards-based APIs with versioning and backward compatibility: Mandatory
- 200+ data source integrations over platform lifetime: Required
- Azure API Management for all internal/external APIs: Mandatory
- Centralised, versioned schema registry with automated compatibility checks: Required

**Cost Optimisation & Financial Governance:**
- Standardised cloud resource tagging (by app, BU, environment, cost centre): Mandatory
- Cloud cost visibility with chargeback/showback reporting: Mandatory
- Cost alerts and guardrails to prevent overruns: Required
- FinOps practices during implementation and AMS: Mandatory

sections.title: Implementation Approach & Methodology
sections.instructions:
Write the Implementation Approach & Methodology section covering overall approach and six implementation stages:

**Overall Delivery Approach (bullet list):**
- Phased implementation methodology progressing from requirements understanding and architecture design through build, test, deploy, and operate
- Balance agility with governance — iterative delivery while maintaining architectural, security, and compliance standards
- Close collaboration with AEML business, IT, security, and data governance teams throughout the engagement
- Leverage industry best practices for data platform, analytics, and AI foundation implementations
- Comprehensive project tracker maintained across all implementation activities; updated regularly and shared with AEML

**Stage 1 — Discovery, Requirement Analysis & Planning:** Requirements workshops with business, functional, data, and technical stakeholders; assess existing Azure/Databricks landscape (integrations, data assets, ingestion mechanisms, security controls); validate and refine scope of SAP and non-SAP source system onboarding (business priorities, data availability/quality/feasibility, regulatory considerations, interdependencies, anticipated additions); define data governance roles and stewardship responsibilities; produce solution design, target architecture, and implementation roadmap with phase-wise onboarding strategy, dependencies, assumptions, and governance checkpoints.

**Stage 2 — Architecture & Design:** Cloud-first, scalable, secure data architecture design; define ingestion patterns, processing frameworks, and storage layers; design Domain/Business Data Model and analytical data models; define security, access control, and compliance mechanisms; finalise CI/CD, monitoring, and operational architecture.

**Stage 3 — Build & Configuration:** Implement data ingestion pipelines for identified SAP and non-SAP systems; develop data processing and transformation workflows; implement data models and semantic layers; configure data governance, metadata, lineage, and data quality capabilities; set up CI/CD pipelines and automation.

**Stage 4 — Validation & Testing:** Comprehensive platform validation — unit, system, integration, performance, and security testing; validate data accuracy, completeness, consistency, reconciliation, and timeliness across all layers; test governance and control mechanisms (metadata, lineage, quality rules, RBAC, audit logging); business stakeholder validation of Domain/Business Data Models, Business Taxonomy and Glossary, and sample KPIs/dashboards; validate MLOps capabilities; document all test scenarios, results, defects, and remediation actions. Formal AEML sign-off required to proceed to deployment.

**Stage 5 — Deployment & Go-Live:** Controlled deployment across environments; production readiness and operational sign-off; VAPT support (conducted per AEML/Adani Group information security policies by AEML or a CERT-In empanelled agency or as mutually agreed); all VAPT security observations must be remediated and evidence submitted before Production Go-Live; Go-Live activities and stabilisation; documentation and knowledge transfer completion. Vendor remains responsible until formal handover and Operational Acceptance by AEML.

**Stage 6 — Hypercare & Transition to Operations:** Post Go-Live Hypercare for 1 month (or as otherwise agreed). Heightened support: faster response and resolution for production issues; continuous monitoring of ingestion, processing, analytics, and platform services; on-demand support to business, technical, and operational users. Address post-Go-Live stabilisation: residual defects, performance tuning, production environment validation; early production usage validation (platform availability/performance meets NFRs; data accuracy and governance controls effective; access controls and audit logging function as intended). Formal transition to AMS on Hypercare completion with Hypercare closure report and sign-off.

sections.title: Governance & Change Management
sections.instructions:
Write the Governance, Change Management, and Quality Assurance section:

**Governance & Quality Management (bullets):**
- Establish project governance structure with defined roles and responsibilities
- Identify, track, and mitigate delivery risks proactively
- Follow quality assurance and review checkpoints across all phases
- Adhere to AEML's architectural, security, and compliance standards
- Submit detailed resource deployment plan (organisation structure, roles, key personnel, skill profiles) subject to AEML approval
- Establish formal escalation matrix with response timelines and stakeholder responsibilities for technical, operational, security, and delivery-related issues
- All project team members must comply with AEML cybersecurity guidelines and data security policies

**Agile Delivery & Progress Reporting (bullets):**
- Adopt Agile or hybrid Agile delivery approach for iterative and incremental delivery
- Provide sprint-wise progress reports: completed activities, ongoing tasks, upcoming deliverables, issues, severity, and resolution timelines
- Obtain incremental sign-off from AEML for agreed deliverables at end of each iteration/phase
- AEML may conduct Sprint Review meetings and senior management review meetings
- Participate in retrospective/corrective action meetings for major deviations or delivery risks

**Change Management (bullets):**
- Formal Change Control Process for bidder-initiated and buyer-initiated changes
- Each change requires written Change Request/Proposal describing: change description; impact on timelines; cost implications; impact on functionality, performance, or guarantees; impact on other contractual provisions
- Changes due to bidder default or non-performance shall not constitute valid change requests and shall not entitle the bidder to time extension or commercial adjustment

**Inspection & Audit:** AEML has the right to review, inspect, and audit implementation deliverables, configurations, and services during the project lifecycle. Bidder shall provide full cooperation during all inspections.

sections.title: Helpdesk Support Services
sections.instructions:
Write the Helpdesk Support Services section:

**Helpdesk Support (prose):** The selected Vendor shall provide a dedicated helpdesk during agreed AEML business hours via multiple channels (email, telephone, remote access). The Vendor shall establish structured Incident, Problem, and Change Management processes aligned with ITIL or equivalent. An escalation matrix with defined response times, resolution timelines, and L1/L2/L3 contacts shall be submitted and agreed during onboarding. All incidents, service requests, and changes shall be logged and tracked through a centralised ticketing mechanism with clear ownership and status visibility for AEML stakeholders.

**Service Review & Governance Meetings (bullets):**
- Monthly service review meetings led by designated Vendor Account Manager
- Agenda: SLA performance and service metrics; incident summary, root causes, and corrective actions; platform performance, availability, and capacity; open issues, risks, and planned changes; continuous improvement and optimisation opportunities
- Minutes of meeting (MoM), action items, and agreed improvement plans documented and shared with AEML
- Meeting outcomes feed continuous service improvement and alignment with AEML strategic objectives

sections.title: Project Plan & Milestones
sections.instructions:
Write the Project Plan section with high-level milestones table and supporting details:

**Introduction (prose):** 'The bidder shall propose a detailed project plan aligned with AEML's governance framework. The project plan shall clearly define milestones, deliverables, timelines, and dependencies.'

**High-Level Project Milestones (markdown pipe table):**

| Stage Gate | Milestone | Planned Timeline | Key Outcomes |
|-----------|-----------|-----------------|---------------|
| PI | Project Initiation | Apr 2026 | Project kickoff, governance and PMO setup, stakeholder alignment, finalisation of delivery model and communication plan |
| PC | Project Commitment | Apr 2026 | Target-state architecture finalisation, detailed solution design, security and compliance alignment, implementation roadmap sign-off |
| PB | Platform Build & Integration | Aug 2026 | Core platform build on Azure-Databricks, data ingestion frameworks, Medallion architecture implementation, domain data models, CI/CD and governance foundations |
| VR | Verified & Ready | Sep 2026 | Completion of system, integration, performance, and security testing; platform stabilisation and readiness confirmation |
| UATR | UAT Executed & Ready | Oct 2026 | Business user acceptance testing completed; UAT sign-off; Go-Live readiness confirmed |
| RO | Rollout (Go-Live) | Nov 2026 | Production Go-Live, activation of dashboards and sample KPIs, start of Hypercare support |
| PCD | Project Closure | Dec 2026 | Knowledge transfer, documentation handover, stabilisation completion, formal transition to AMS/steady-state operations |

**Key Deliverables by Phase (bullets):** Approved solution architecture and design documents; implemented data ingestion pipelines for identified systems; data models and semantic layers for analytics; configured governance, security, and access controls; operational dashboards and monitoring setup; limited KPIs/dashboards for platform validation; documentation and knowledge transfer materials.

**Dependencies & Assumptions (bullets):** Timely availability of source system access and data; availability of Azure infrastructure and enterprise tools; active participation from AEML stakeholders; decisions and approvals within agreed timelines.

**Reporting & Review (bullets):** Weekly and monthly status reports; regular governance and review meeting participation; risk, issue, and dependency tracking; milestone progress visibility.

Note: 'Timelines are indicative and based on AEML's current planning assumptions. The bidder shall submit a detailed project plan as part of the proposal. AEML reserves the right to modify timelines based on project priorities.'

sections.title: Support, Maintenance & Operations — AMS
sections.instructions:
Write the Annual Maintenance & Support section:

**Introduction (prose):** The selected bidder shall provide comprehensive AMS services to ensure optimal performance, stability, and availability of the deployed solution for 3 years following Hypercare completion and Operational Acceptance. AMS commences post Go-Live and Hypercare upon AEML's grant of Operational Acceptance following successful UAT.

**AMS Services Included (bullets):**
- L2/L3 incident and problem management; escalation coordination with OEMs and partners
- 24×7×365 platform monitoring, operations control, and alerting (Databricks jobs, clusters, storage, APIs, integrations)
- Performance tuning and optimisation (Spark job optimisation, query performance, cost efficiency)
- Security patch management: critical ≤ 48 hours, high ≤ 7 days, medium/low ≤ 1 month
- Minor enhancements, configuration changes, and optimisations within agreed AMS scope and effort thresholds — without additional commercial approval
- Support for onboarding additional users, reports, and data sources within agreed scope limits
- Monthly SLA reports: incident/issue summary, uptime and availability matrix, RCA for major incidents, SLA compliance status
- Quarterly performance review meetings with AEML stakeholders
- Backup, recovery, and DR support; periodic backup restorability validation; DR drill coordination
- Security operations support: RBAC/IAM/access reviews, encryption compliance, audit log review, compliance reporting
- Continuous improvement recommendations aligned with industry best practices and AEML's digital roadmap

**AMS Team Requirements (bullets):**
- Dedicated AMS Manager as SPOC for all buyer interactions, issue management, governance, cross-team coordination, and status communication
- Data Engineers proficient in PySpark/Spark SQL, Bronze-Silver-Gold architecture, Delta Lake, batch/streaming pipelines, data quality, CI/CD, and Git
- Team includes: Technical Support Engineers, Azure Databricks Experts, Functional Experts, Business Analysts, Integration/API Engineers, Incident & Problem Management Specialists
- AMS staff found unsatisfactory by AEML shall be replaced within 7 calendar days

**Exit Management (bullets):**
- Structured exit and transition plan at end of AMS contract
- All knowledge, documentation, source code, configurations, and credentials handed over without dependency
- Assist smooth transition to alternative vendor or internal team without operational disruption
- Knowledge transfer sessions during transition period

sections.title: Service Level Agreement — Platform Availability & Performance
sections.instructions:
Write the SLA section with an introductory sentence and then the full SLA definitions table:

Introduction: 'This Service Level Agreement (SLA) defines the minimum service levels, performance standards, availability targets, and support obligations to be adhered to by the selected Vendor during implementation, Hypercare, and the subsequent support and maintenance period for the platform at AEML.'

Generate the SLA definitions as a comprehensive markdown pipe table:

| SLA Category | Metric | Target / Threshold | Measurement / Notes |
|-------------|--------|-------------------|---------------------|
| Platform Uptime | Monthly uptime for core platform components (ingestion, processing, storage, analytics, APIs, governance services) | ≥ 99.95% | Excluding pre-approved scheduled maintenance (communicated ≥ 48 hours in advance, preferably during non-business hours) |
| Data Pipeline Availability | Successful pipeline execution rate per month | ≥ 99.95% | (Successful runs ÷ Total scheduled runs) × 100. Excluding approved maintenance |
| Batch Data Freshness | Data availability from batch pipelines | Within T+X hours as defined per pipeline | Per-pipeline SLA to be agreed during design phase onboarding |
| Streaming Data Freshness | End-to-end latency for streaming pipelines | ≤ 15 minutes (unless otherwise defined per pipeline) | Breach = delay exceeding agreed SLA for two consecutive runs |
| Dashboard & Semantic Model Response Time | Standard analytics dashboards, semantic models, and catalog searches | Load within 2–5 seconds under normal operating conditions | |
| Concurrent Load Performance | Platform performance under peak load | No more than 10% degradation under 2× peak concurrent user load | Load test verification required |
| Data Quality Score | Critical datasets per agreed DQ rules | ≥ 98% (Valid records ÷ Total records) | Critical DQ failure resolution: ≤ 8 business hours |
| Scalability | Data source integrations | Support 200+ data source integrations; growth in users, workloads, BUs without architectural redesign | Configuration-driven, cloud-native scaling |
| Backup & Recovery — RTO | Recovery time after disaster/failure | ≤ 60 minutes | Tested through DR drills; all backup data encrypted |
| Backup & Recovery — RPO | Data loss window in disaster scenario | ≤ 30 minutes | Backup retained per AEML data retention policies |
| Security Patches — Critical | Application of critical security patches | Within 48 hours of identification | |
| Security Patches — High | Application of high security patches | Within 7 days of identification | |
| Security Patches — Medium/Low | Application of medium/low security patches | Within 1 month of identification | |
| Pipeline Enhancement Turnaround | Minor pipeline enhancement/optimisation completion | ≤ 5 business days (effort ≤ agreed AMS threshold) | Acknowledgement within 1 business day |
| CI/CD Reliability | Vendor code-related CI/CD pipeline failure resolution | Within 1 business day | |
| Version Rollback | Platform version rollback capability | Within 30 minutes | |

sections.title: SLA — Incident Management & Support Response
sections.instructions:
Generate the Incident Management SLA as a markdown pipe table followed by additional obligations:

| Severity | Description | Initial Response | Resolution Target |
|----------|-------------|-----------------|-------------------|
| Critical | Platform outage / major data failure / all-users-impacted incident | ≤ 2 hours | ≤ 4 hours |
| High | Significant functionality degradation or critical data quality failure affecting business-critical processes | ≤ 2 hours | ≤ 8 hours |
| Medium | Business-impacting degradation or partial functionality loss affecting specific users or functions | ≤ 2 hours | ≤ 24 hours |
| Low | Minor defects, cosmetic issues, or non-critical enhancements | ≤ 2 hours | ≤ 3 business days |

Additional obligations (bullets):
- Vendor shall provide ticketing system, escalation matrix, and L2/L3 support during contracted support period
- Root Cause Analysis (RCA) for Critical and recurring incidents shall be shared with AEML within 5 business days of incident closure
- Support coverage during AEML business hours with extended/24×7 coverage for Critical incidents

sections.title: SLA Breach & Penalties
sections.instructions:
Generate the SLA Breach & Penalties section with three tables and a penalty cap note:

**Table 1 — Uptime & Data Pipeline-Based Penalties (Monthly):**
| Uptime & Successful Pipeline Execution Rate Achieved | Penalty (% of Quarterly AMC) |
|------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| 99.5% – 99.94% | 1% |
| 99.0% – 99.49% | 2% |
| < 99.0% | 5% |

**Table 2 — Resolution SLA Breach Penalties:**
| SLA Breach | Penalty |
|-----------|----------|
| Critical issue not resolved within 4 hours | 5% of Quarterly AMC |
| High issue not resolved within 8 hours | 3% of Quarterly AMC |
| Medium issue not resolved within 24 hours | 2% of Quarterly AMC |
| Low issue not resolved within 3 business days (72 hours) | 1% of Quarterly AMC |
| Initial response (any severity) not provided within 2 hours | 1% of Quarterly AMC |

**Table 3 — Additional SLA Violations:**
| Violation | Penalty |
|----------|---------|
| Failure to submit monthly SLA reports on time | 1% of Quarterly AMC |
| DR breach (RTO/RPO deviation without justified cause and documented evidence) | 1% of Quarterly AMC |

**Penalty Cap & Recovery:**
- Total penalties capped at 5% of Quarterly AMC per month
- Penalties deducted from quarterly AMC invoice
- Where SLA breaches are attributable to AEML or third parties, corresponding relief provided subject to documented evidence and mutual agreement

Note: 'SLA penalties are intended to ensure accountability, reliability, and service quality — not to be punitive in nature.'

sections.title: Appendix — Data Source Mapping (A1)
sections.instructions:
Generate the Data Sources appendix table listing the key systems to be integrated or considered for integration. Format as markdown pipe table:

| # | Business Unit | Function | Sub-Process | Data Source / System | Type | Description | Connected to Existing Data Lake | Roadmap |
|---|---------------|----------|-------------|---------------------|------|-------------|--------------------------------|----------|

Generate a representative table of 20-25 major data sources from across the following categories (extract exact details from source documents):

**SAP Systems:** SAP ECC/S4 (ERP), SAP ISU (Meter/Billing/Collection), SAP BW-BO (Data Warehouse), SAP BPC (Financial reporting), SAP C4C (CRM), SAP HR, SAP SOLMAN, SAP MDG, SAP GRC, SAP Ariba

**Commercial/CRM:** MS CRM, Network Recommendation Portal, New Connection Portal, Collections App, Billing App, Change of Name Portal, CCM (Customer Communication Management), BDTS (Bill Distribution Tracking)

**Network/OT/SCADA:** SCADA/ADMS (via staging server), OMS (Outage Management System), DSS (Demand Scheduling System), Feeder Demand Portal, ArcGIS/GIS, WMS (Work Management System)

**Smart Meter & MDM:** Smart Meter APIs (8 instances), HES (OBIS code-wise smart meter data), Esyasoft MDM, MDM (Smart Meter Data), Meter Download Handheld, Adani ConnectX Cloud

**Customer Channels:** AEML ChatBot, Krista Bot (Conversational AI), Avaya (Voice Recording via RPA), IEX (Power Exchange APIs)

**HR & Admin:** Oracle HCM (Payroll), Kronos (Attendance), Percipio (Learning), Gensuite (Safety/ESG), Genetec (Physical Security)

**Project Management:** Primavera (EPC Projects/IPMS), eNFA (NFA Approvals), OpenText (Document Management)

**Analytics:** SAP BW-BO (existing), SQL Theobald (Staging server), DAS (Data Authenticated System)

For each row: use 'First', 'Second', or 'Third-Party' for Type column; use 'Connected' or 'Not Connected' for data lake status; use dates from source documents (e.g., Mar-25, May-26, Aug-26) for Roadmap.

Add note: 'This list is indicative. Final scope of data source onboarding shall be validated and agreed during Stage 1 — Discovery & Requirement Analysis. AEML reserves the right to add, remove, or reprioritise data sources during the course of implementation based on business priorities and technical feasibility.'
